race.name: Mammoth Marathon
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers at 8,000 feet in the Eastern Sierra — the highest-altitude Nordic marathon in America, where thin air meets deep snow.

vitals.aid_stations: 3-4 stations with water, sports drink, snacks

climate.description: At 2,440 meters, Mammoth sits in one of the snowiest locations in California. The Eastern Sierra receives both Pacific storms and cold continental air. Race day temperatures range from -15°C to 0°C. Intense high-altitude sun and thin air are defining features.
challenges: Intense UV exposure at high elevation
Thin air reduces performance 10-15% for lowland skiers
Pacific storms can bring heavy snowfall during race week
Temperature inversions can create extreme cold in valley bottom

course.primary: Loops through the Tamarack Cross Country Ski Center trails and surrounding Sierra terrain. The course winds through lodgepole pine forest and open meadows with views of the Sherwin Range, Mammoth Mountain, and the eastern escarpment of the Sierra Nevada.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through Sierra forest and meadow terrain
course.grooming: Good grooming by Tamarack Cross Country Ski Center, well-maintained classic and skate tracks
features: Highest-altitude Nordic marathon in the United States (2,440m / 8,000ft)
Stunning Eastern Sierra backdrop — Sherwin Range and Mammoth Mountain
Reliable deep snowpack from Sierra storms
One of the oldest Nordic marathons in the American West
Combined with other winter events during Mammoth winter season

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: The highest-altitude Nordic marathon in the US, with near-guaranteed snow and spectacular Sierra scenery. Nearly 50 years of history gives it gravitas, though field size remains modest. Altitude is both a feature and a challenge.

history.summary: Founded in 1978, the Mammoth Marathon is one of the oldest Nordic ski marathons in the American West. It grew out of the Mammoth Lakes cross-country skiing community, which has thrived due to the area's legendary snowfall — the Eastern Sierra routinely receives over 10 meters of snow annually. The race has persisted for nearly five decades despite the challenges of organizing a high-altitude winter event, becoming a fixture of the California Nordic scene.
notable_facts: Founded in 1978 — one of the oldest Nordic marathons in the western US
At 2,440m, it is the highest-altitude Nordic marathon in America
Mammoth Lakes receives an average of 10+ meters of snowfall annually
The Eastern Sierra has been a training ground for US Ski Team members
The Tamarack Cross Country Ski Center has operated since the 1970s

videos.title: In-Depth - Mammoth 2022 Altitude Running Camp
videos.description:
In-Depth Mammoth 2022 is a two-part episodic series that was recorded during our time at Mammoth Lakes in August 2022. We interviewed veteran coaches from Loyola High, Mira Costa High, Crescenta Valley High, and Tesoro High and covered various topics.

In this episode we focus on Altitude Running Camp -- a tradition for many high school cross country programs in California.  Rich Gonzalez of PrepCalTrack delves into questions such as "why Mammoth altitude camp?", "what's a typical day", "who attends?", and "how long has the team had this tradition?".  Bonding is a key outcome.
tags: Crescenta Valley High School Cross Country

videos.title: Canadian XC Team High Altitude Camp in Mammoth
videos.description: Training Camp in Mammoth, California with the Canadian Ski Team. Guest Peter Larsson of Sweden.
videos.curation_reason: Shows actual cross-country skiing training content in Mammoth with the Canadian Ski Team.
